message:
fix(packs/system/services): close arg-as-subcommand bypass in systemctl safe patterns

systemctl safe patterns used `.*?\s+<sub>(?=\s|$)`. A unit name literally
matching the safe subcommand would short-circuit destructive ops:

  systemctl stop status      -> matched systemctl-status safe
  systemctl disable show     -> matched systemctl-show safe
  systemctl mask cat         -> matched systemctl-cat safe

Replaced `.*?` with flag-walker `(?:\s+--?\S+(?:\s+\S+)?)*` on all 6
systemctl safe subcommands (status, list-*, show, is-*, daemon-reload,
cat). Global flags (`systemctl -H host status`, `systemctl --user show`)
continue to work.
